statistics.name: assists
statistics.description: The number of times a player who passes the ball to a teammate in a way that leads to a score by field goal, meaning that he or she was "assisting" in the basket. There is some judgment involved in deciding whether a passer should be credited with an assist.

articles.headline: How every five-star men's college basketball commit fits at his new school
articles.description: Breaking down top class of 2026 commitments, including a third five-star recruit for BYU in three years.

images.name: Bruce Branch III [1296x729]
images.caption: Bruce Branch III, the former No. 2 prospect who reclassified up from the 2027 class, has committed to BYU, giving Kevin Young a third consecutive class featuring a five-star recruit.

images.name: Jordan Smith [1296x729]
images.caption: Jordan Smith is the second five-star recruit of the 2026 high school class to commit to Arkansas.

images.name: Cameron Williams commit fit
images.caption: A top-3 recruit in the class of 2026, Cameron Williams is headed to Durham to play for Jon Scheyer and the Duke Blue Devils.

images.name: Jason Crowe [1144x644]
images.caption: Jason Crowe, No. 4 recruit in the 2026 ESPN 100 player rankings, has committed to Missouri.

images.name: Babatunde Oladotun [1066x600]
images.caption: Babatunde Oladotun, the former 2027 No. 1 prospect who reclassified to the 2026 class and is ranked in the top-10, has announced his commitment to Maryland.

images.name: Dylan Mingo [1296x729]
images.caption: Premier class of 2026 guard Dylan Mingo has committed to North Carolina for the 2026-27 season.

images.name: Caleb Gaskins [1296x729]
images.caption: Caleb Gaskins is staying in-state next season to play for Miami and first-time head coach Jai Lucas.

images.name: Deron Rippey Jr. [1296x729]
images.caption: Deron Rippey Jr. has committed to Duke, giving the Blue Devils another top recruit.

images.name: Toni Bryant (commitment photo) [600x338]
images.caption: Toni Bryant, the No. 14 recruit in the class of 2026, has committed to Missouri, giving the Tigers two five-star prospects for the 2026-27 season.

images.name: Austin Goosby (Texas) [1296x729]
images.caption: Austin Goosby has committed to Sean Miller and Texas for the 2026-27 season.

images.name: Taylen Kinney [1296x729]
images.caption: Taylen Kinney is one of the best point guards in the boys' SC Next class of 2026, and recently committed to Kansas.
images.credit: Brian Rothmuller/Icon Sportswire via Getty Images

article.headline: Estrella and Okpara lead No. 23 Tennessee to dominant 78-59 win over South Carolina
article.description: — J.P. Estrella scored 22 points, Felix Okpara added 20 and No. 23 Tennessee rolled past South Carolina 78-59 on Tuesday night to stop a two-game skid.
article.linkText: Estrella and Okpara lead No. 23 Tennessee to dominant 78-59 win over South Carolina

images.name: Highlight: J.P. Estrella powers No. 23 Tennessee to win over South Carolina
images.caption: Estrella leads the way with 22 points and seven rebounds as Felix Okpara puts up 20 points to give the Volunteers a 78-59 victory against the Gamecocks.

video.headline: Highlight: J.P. Estrella powers No. 23 Tennessee to win over South Carolina
video.shortHeadline: Highlight: J.P. Estrella powers No. 23 Tennessee to win over South Carolina
video.caption: Estrella leads the way with 22 points and seven rebounds as Felix Okpara puts up 20 points to give the Volunteers a 78-59 victory against the Gamecocks.
video.title: Highlight: J.P. Estrella powers No. 23 Tennessee to win over South Carolina
video.description: Estrella leads the way with 22 points and seven rebounds as Felix Okpara puts up 20 points to give the Volunteers a 78-59 victory against the Gamecocks.
video.shortDescription: Estrella leads the way with 22 points and seven rebounds as Felix Okpara puts up 20 points to give the Volunteers a 78-59 victory against the Gamecocks.

article.story: COLUMBIA, S.C. -- — J.P. Estrella scored 22 points, Felix Okpara added 20 and No. 23 Tennessee rolled past South Carolina 78-59 on Tuesday night to stop a two-game skid. The Volunteers (21-9, 11-6 Southeastern Conference) were a force inside, even without injured starter and second-leading scorer Nate Ament, a 6-foot-10 freshman. Tennessee shook off Ament's absence with post players Estrella and Okpara dismantling South Carolina's defense with dunks and layups. Ja'Kobi Gillespie, the Vols' leading scorer at 18.7 points per game, was held to eight points on 3-of-9 shooting. But he had a career-high 12 assists as he regularly found Estrella and Okpara close to the basket. The Vols held a 56-18 scoring edge in the paint as Estrella and Okpara combined to make 20 of 27 shots. Meechie Johnson, who was honored on senior night, scored 20 points to lead the Gamecocks (12-18, 3-14), who’ve lost 10 of 11. The Vols took a 21-10 lead eight minutes in and continued pounding the ball inside. South Carolina had no answers as Tennessee's big men lurked near the basket awaiting passes. South Carolina cut the Vols' lead to 46-40 on Kobe Knox's basket with 16 minutes left. But Estrella had three more inside buckets during Tennessee's 10-4 charge to take control. Tennessee shot 54% (33 of 61) from the field, its highest percentage in an SEC game this season. The Vols remain in the hunt for a double-bye in the league tournament with one regular-season game left. Up next Tennessee: Hosts No. 24 Vanderbilt on Saturday. South Carolina: Ends the regular season at Mississippi on Saturday. ------ Get poll alerts and updates on the AP Top 25 throughout the season. Sign up here and here (AP News mobile app). AP college basketball: https://apnews.com/hub/ap-top-25-college-basketball-poll and https://apnews.com/hub/college-basketball
